one_time_description: Chief and Headperson Settlement proceeds of $2,634,605 less negotiation costs of $403,593 and per capita distributions of $2,131,920, netting to $99,092
economic_notes: First Nation is located in Treaty 6 Territory, Saskatchewan. Has litigation financing for multiple claims including Husky Oil Spill, Treaty Rights, Highway & Gravel, and Sweetgrass Railway. Member of BATC Investments Limited Partnership (20%), FN Cannabis Limited Partnership (33%), and BATC Land Holdings Limited Partnership (16.67%). Sweetgrass Developments Limited Partnership and Sweetgrass Investments Ltd. are consolidated entities.
notes: Fiscal year ends March 31, 2023 - this is the 2023 fiscal year, not 2024. Consolidated entities include Sweetgrass First Nation, Sweetgrass First Nation C.M.H.C. Housing, Sweetgrass Developments Limited Partnership, and Sweetgrass Investments Ltd. Sweetgrass Treaty Land Entitlement Trust and Sweetgrass Legacy Trust are excluded from consolidation as they are not controlled by Chief and Council. Auditor's report includes an Emphasis of Matter regarding litigation financing for multiple claims. New accounting standards adopted in 2023: PS 3450 Financial Instruments and PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations, resulting in recognition of $1,434,103 ARO liability. One-time Chief and Headperson settlement of $2,634,605 received with offsetting costs. All deferred revenue relates to ISC funding. ISC total government transfers calculated as ISC net ($9,879,459) plus CMHC ($341,803) plus SITAG ($191,082) plus Class Action settlements ($500,000 + $89,998) = $11,002,342 but note SITAG and Class Action may not be government transfers in traditional sense; total_government_transfers set to ISC + CMHC + SITAG + Class Action settlements = 10562634.
